title: How to Keep Shipping When You Walk Away from Your Desk — Zack Proser, WorkOS
summary: Zack Proser describes a workflow system that dispatches agents via voice briefs to isolated git worktrees while the user is offline, using signal agents to monitor Slack and Linear, verification gates for code quality and behavior, weekly analysis of conversation history for skill refinement, and biometric feedback integration via MCP. The design prioritizes asynchronous execution and human attention preservation rather than raw tool speed.
writeup: Zack Proser of WorkOS walks through a workflow system that keeps agents shipping while the operator is away from the desk. Voice briefs dispatch agents into isolated git worktrees, signal agents monitor Slack and Linear, and verification gates check code quality and behavior before work lands. The system also runs weekly analysis of conversation history to refine agent skills and integrates biometric feedback through MCP. The stated priority is asynchronous execution and preserving human attention rather than maximizing raw tool speed.
